purpose: Configurable EXPOSITION register for the deep-research-survey skill. The `audience` axis (SKILL.md, Modes and flags) selects a register; `audience: <value>` overrides per run. EDIT THE PER-KNOB VALUES HERE to retune without touching SKILL.md or the phase files. Phase-1 resolves the register; R-GOV (addenda/phase-2.md) reads it for the fundamentals floor; R-DEPTH (addenda/phase-4.md) reads it for exposition. This file is the single source of truth.
axis_note: Register modulates EXPOSITION (how prose is written for a reader) and is orthogonal to BOTH the throughput axis (config/operational-scale.json) AND the depth-tier axis (R-GOV headline/load-bearing/catalog). Register decides HOW a concept is written; R-GOV decides WHICH concepts get deep treatment; scale decides evidence throughput. A headline concept can be written learner- or expert-style — so register is independent of depth tier.
default_note: `practitioner` reproduces the skill's current Staff-level register exactly — shipping default-on for it is a no-op. Pass `audience: learner` or `audience: expert` only when the reader differs.

prerequisite_floor.learner: derive/define prerequisites from first principles before using them (e.g., what a softmax or a KL divergence is)
prerequisite_floor.practitioner: assume working domain ML / deep-learning math; cite or forward-reference prerequisites
prerequisite_floor.expert: assume fluency; relegate prerequisites to the notation contract / glossary
prerequisite_floor.note: what the reader is assumed to already know.

routine_algebra.learner: show every algebra step with a one-line justification
routine_algebra.practitioner: show the key steps; name (not expand) standard manipulations
routine_algebra.expert: compress routine manipulation ('standard manipulation gives ...'); keep only the load-bearing moves
routine_algebra.note: GOVERNED BY THE INVARIANT: compression touches ROUTINE algebra only, never a load-bearing logical step (see register_invariants).

intuition_density.learner: intuition + analogy before AND after each non-obvious result
intuition_density.practitioner: one intuition box per load-bearing result
intuition_density.expert: one tight intuition line per result
intuition_density.note: the intuition itself is the same; only how much surrounds the math changes.

worked_example_role.learner: the worked example LEADS — motivate the formula with a concrete instance first
worked_example_role.practitioner: the worked example FOLLOWS the derivation as a self-checking oracle
worked_example_role.expert: the worked example is a terse numeric sanity check / oracle
worked_example_role.note: the example's numbers are IDENTICAL across registers (register_invariants); only its placement/framing changes.

term_definitions.learner: define every non-trivial term on first use, inline
term_definitions.practitioner: define non-standard terms; standard terms assumed
term_definitions.expert: notation-contract / glossary only; no inline definitions

fundamentals_depth.learner: treat fundamentals / prerequisite concepts at FULL pedagogical depth (pin to `headline` tier)
fundamentals_depth.practitioner: tier the fundamentals on merit, per R-GOV as usual
fundamentals_depth.expert: fundamentals at catalog/recap depth — assume known; concentrate the depth budget on advanced material
fundamentals_depth.note: the R-GOV interaction: register sets the fundamentals FLOOR. It changes which tier the basics get, never the depth-coverage-fraction SCORING (R-RUBRIC stays register-blind).

register_invariants: every boxed / load-bearing result is identical across registers (same equation, same boxed form)
every worked-example / oracle number is identical across registers (the WORKED-EXAMPLE [C3] values do not move)
every epistemic-status and prediction tag is identical across registers

the no-load-bearing-step-dropped floor holds in EVERY register: `expert` compresses ROUTINE connective algebra only, never a load-bearing logical step (math-authoring.md 'do not skip steps' applies to load-bearing steps always)
the citation-integrity gate and the mechanical gate (lint-math / validate-refs / check-citation-sources) are register-blind
R-MATHREV, R-COVER, and R-RUBRIC scoring are register-blind — coverage-fraction over load-bearing items, not prose volume
invariant_rule: An `audience` register is INVALID if it would change any boxed result, any worked-oracle number, any epistemic/prediction tag, or drop a load-bearing derivation step. Register changes EXPOSITION, never correctness. `expert` adds nothing false by compressing routine algebra; `learner` adds connective tissue and basics, never a new claim. A register that fails an invariant is a bug, not a style.
resolution_precedence: 2. else explicit register language in the request ('for someone learning the field' -> learner; 'expert-terse' / 'for specialists' -> expert)
3. else default to `practitioner` (current behavior); ask only if the audience would materially change the fundamentals depth and the request is silent
override_syntax: audience: learner   |   audience: practitioner   |   audience: expert
report_footer_rule: Record the active audience register in the Phase-5 report footer next to mode/flags/scale.
